SÜLEYMAN BÜLBÜL (Aydın) - Thank you, President.
These regulations - I'm talking about both 14 and 15 - mean removing the practice that considers the senior public prosecutor in the relevant regional court of justice to be the deputy chief prosecutor, and instead making this appointment by the HSK debatable in terms of merit, experience, concern about politicization, legal security, and professional principles. Because these new deputy chief prosecutors will be appointed by a politicized HSK, they will examine the actions of other chief public prosecutors regarding their judicial and administrative duties and inform the chief public prosecutor.
In addition, in the justification of the proposal, while it was considered a drawback for senior public prosecutors to leave office frequently due to reasons such as retirement or appointment, the HSK also had the authority to change these deputy chief prosecutors frequently. Considering the politicized structure of the HSK and the many appointment and dismissal choices it has used to date, the frequent change of deputy chief prosecutors due to a critical investigation and prosecution may make it possible for senior public prosecutors in the region to face new pressures. Appointing a first-class judge or prosecutor determined by the HSK to this position, instead of the experience of the most senior public prosecutor in the region, also carries the danger of creating new doubts in the public opinion on some places and cases.
Mr. President, at the point where the judiciary is in our country - where HSK is at - the biggest concern for us is the politicization of the judiciary. Politicization of the judiciary means, for example, Akın Gürlek becoming Deputy Minister, and after being Deputy Minister, he goes to the Istanbul Chief Public Prosecutor's Office. Politicization of the judiciary means that Hasan Yılmaz - a chief prosecutor who had a request in the injunction decision of the SBK, the Deputy Chief Public Prosecutor of Istanbul - was later appointed to the position of Deputy Minister - after the SBK escaped - and then from the position of Deputy Minister to the member of the Supreme Court of Appeals. So, there is a fact in this context: The judiciary is becoming politicized, and HSK is an institution within this politicization. We need to create ways to eliminate this, so the concrete reasons are clear.
Now, we have data in the European Court of Human Rights. According to these data, as of the end of February 2024, 35 out of every 100 cases pending at ECHR consist of applications made by Turkish citizens. When we look at it this way, here are the ratios of the number of files pending in the European Court of Human Rights; our 35 out of 100 cases, Russia 16, Ukraine 12.9; Romania 6; Greece 3.6; Italy 3.6; Serbia 2.3; Azerbaijan 3.1; The other 37 countries are 13.2. Now, when we consider the 49 countries that are members of the European Court of Human Rights, 35 cases out of these 100 clearly demonstrate that the right to a fair trial has been violated in the judiciary in Turkey. Again, according to ECHR data, Türkiye has been convicted mostly for violation of the right to a fair trial and violation of the right to security and freedom in 2023. So, if there is a violation of the right to a fair trial in 2023, it means that something is wrong in the judiciary. What are these things going wrong?

Again, Türkiye is the country where the European Court of Human Rights has given the most precedent decisions as of 2023, friends, the country where precedent decisions have been made is Türkiye. In the international community, 124 precedent cases are known as Türkiye decisions. Now, one, 35 out of 100 cases; two, violation of the right to a fair trial and violation of the right to security and liberty; Turkey in AHİM in 2023; Three, the country with the most precedent decisions is Türkiye, 124 Turkish precedent decisions. What does this entail? This means that the decisions made in our first instance judiciary and institutions such as the Supreme Court and the Council of State are in violation of the right to a fair trial. In other words, there are citizens of the Republic of Türkiye who cannot get their rights through domestic law. Where is it going? He exercises his right to individual application to the highest point of the judiciary, the Constitutional Court. Türkiye has exceeded 100 thousand cases in exercising the right to individual application. In this context, the Ministry of Justice is looking for solutions regarding individual applications. So there is a problem. When we investigate the reasons for this problem, it is, of course, the politicization of the judiciary.
Friends, we come from districts, we come from provinces, who can say that district presidencies, provincial presidencies or those in politics in certain parties do not influence the judiciary? In the transformations we made regarding the structure of the HSK and the structure of judges and prosecutors in the 27th and 26th Term, did we not enable a lawyer who is a member of a political party or a law school graduate to become a judge or prosecutor? We provided. How does this work? When a judge or prosecutor runs for mayor or a member of parliament and does not win, is it easy for him to return? It doesn't work, he almost can't come back. But he becomes a municipal council member of a political party, a member of a political party, a provincial or district manager of a political party, takes the judgeship exam, and then becomes a judge or prosecutor after the interview. Here, these are our concerns. You are political. Last term, we also had concerns about judges and prosecutors being appointed as senior judges by lottery in the formation of election boards. What was our primary concern? Our concern was that this judge, who is a member of a political party, was appointed to head the election boards upon the application of prosecutors and through lottery or by setting aside the principle of seniority. We repeat our concerns there exactly. In other words, the judiciary has become politicized; Some of the judges and prosecutors are judges and prosecutors who come from certain political party memberships, and now judges and prosecutors make decisions, especially in political and opposition cases, by looking at the palace. This is clear and obvious. Just now, dear brother İnan.
ŞENGÜL KARSLI (Istanbul) - There is nothing clear, admit it.
SÜLEYMAN BÜLBÜL (Aydın) - But I'm telling you.
ŞENGÜL KARSLI (Istanbul) - But everything.
SÜLEYMAN BÜLBÜL (Aydın) - I am giving examples.
ŞENGÜL KARSLI (Istanbul) - You didn't give us any examples, you just made generalizations.
SÜLEYMAN BÜLBÜL (Aydın) - Look, I am giving names, I am telling the Akın Gürlek incident clearly. One, Selahattin Demirtaş case; two, Canan Kaftancıoğlu case, three; Spokesman case; four.
ŞENGÜL KARSLI (Istanbul) - You are the one trying to influence the judiciary.
SÜLEYMAN BÜLBÜL (Aydın) - But I continue, look.

ŞENGÜL KARSLI (Istanbul) - If you have the right to question the decisions of judges here, what kind of a free and independent judiciary is?
SÜLEYMAN BÜLBÜL (Aydın) - Ms. Şengül, look. But I'm giving you an example.
ŞENGÜL KARSLI (Istanbul) - How can you claim that this is a non-neutral attitude based on the results of the case that you do not like?
SÜLEYMAN BÜLBÜL (Aydın) - This was Akın Gürlek, the head of the mobile court at Çağlayan Courthouse. Enis Berberoğlu case. As the chairman of the traveling committee in these cases.
ŞENGÜL KARSLI (Istanbul) - Just because you do not like the results does not mean that it is not free.
SÜLEYMAN BÜLBÜL (Aydın) - Parliament here means the place where people talk; Parliamentarian also means the person who speaks. Please Ms. Şengül, please, I'm telling you clearly.
ŞENGÜL KARSLI (Istanbul) - It is also his duty to tell the truth. But what you said.
İNAN AKGÜN ALP (Kars) - All FETO-supporting judges and prosecutors defended their decisions like this at the time. All FETO judges were making the same defense. “The judiciary is free.” he said, “It is independent.” "No one should discuss these decisions," he said. he said. Now you've come, you're doing the same thing yourself.
SÜLEYMAN BÜLBÜL (Aydın) - Am I the one who sent the judge in the Ekrem İmamoğlu case to Samsun? Please, please.
MAYOR CÜNEYT Yüksel - Dear MPs, dear MPs. Please ask for a word.
ŞENGÜL KARSLI (Istanbul) - “Judges should be impartial and independent.” You say, you judge the decisions they make. Decisions you don't like.
İNAN AKGÜN ALP (Kars) - We are not judging the decision, we are talking about the appointment.
MAYOR CÜNEYT Yüksel - Mr. Karslı. Mr Alp.
SÜLEYMAN BÜLBÜL (Aydın) - We are in the Parliament, Mr. Karslı.
FAHRETTIN TUĞRUL (Uşak) -What are you shouting, why are you shouting?
CHAIRMAN CÜNEYT Yüksel - Mr. Alp.
FAHRETTIN TUĞRUL (Uşak) -Why are you shouting?
İNAN AKGÜN ALP (Kars) - You cannot intervene like that. If you shout, I will shout. “Why are you shouting?” You will tell your deputy first.
FAHRETTİN TUĞRUL (Uşak) - Calm down!
MAYOR CÜNEYT YÜKSEL - Friends, friends, let Mr. Bülbül speak.
FAHRETTİN TUĞRUL (Uşak) -There is a female MP in front of you, be respectful. You keep shouting about women's rights. There is a female MP in front of you, be respectful, you keep shouting!
İNAN AKGÜN ALP (Kars) - I am talking to the MP, this is not a case between men and women.
FAHRETTİN TUĞRUL (Uşak) -This is not the place to shout.
İNAN AKGÜN ALP (Kars) - What do you mean, “This is not the place to shout?”
ŞENGÜL KARSLI (Istanbul) - Damaging the trust in justice is the biggest harm you do to this society, it is injustice. Don't do this rudely either.
MAYOR CÜNEYT Yüksel - Dear Karslı, I will give you my word.
SÜLEYMAN BÜLBÜL (Aydın) - Friends, as I said, Parliament means the place where people talk, and parliamentarian means the person who speaks. We have to express the will of the people, the citizens' screams of injustice, and the violations of the rule of law here. We won't say it here, where will we say it, friends, where will we say it?
NURETTİN ALAN (Istanbul) - Dear Bülbül, ongoing cases cannot be discussed. In Article 138 of the Constitution, the Parliament in pending cases.
SÜLEYMAN BÜLBÜL (Aydın) - Dear Alan, these are the cases that have passed.

İNAN AKGÜN ALP (Kars) - What he is talking about is not the current case, he is talking about the decision to appoint a chief prosecutor. It is not an ongoing case and he is talking about the prosecutor general's previous actions. They are two different things, do not confuse them; He's talking about political appointment.
SÜLEYMAN BÜLBÜL (Aydın) - Friends.
NURETTİN ALAN (Istanbul) - Talking about the İmamoğlu case.
İNAN AKGÜN ALP (Kars) - He is talking about politically motivated judicial intervention. Don't talk about it.
CHAIRMAN CÜNEYT YÜKSEL - Okay, Mr. Alan, let's not talk to each other, please.
SÜLEYMAN BÜLBÜL (Aydın) - Friends.
MAYOR CÜNEYT Yüksel - Dear Alan, I will give you my word.
SÜLEYMAN BÜLBÜL (Aydın) - Friends, this article is the 14th and 15th article. What is in the 14th and 15th articles? There is a new regulation regarding the appointment of deputy chief public prosecutors to BAM. What is it so far? Those who were senior in BAM became deputy chief prosecutors, but now it is said that they are "appointed by the HSK". Prosecutors who are first class or have first class qualifications and who have not worked at BAM will be appointed to BAM if they have been prosecutors for a certain period of time. Who will appoint? HSK will appoint. You pushed seniority aside. We say merit here, we reveal our concerns about the politicization of the judiciary. We will not talk about this here, we will not talk about Akın Gürlek, the head of the traveling delegation said, "I do not recognize the decision of the Constitutional Court" given in the Enis Berberoğlu case. We will not talk about the judge who said, we will not talk about the head of the traveling delegation in 7 political cases in the Çağlayan Courthouse, and this person will enter after being rewarded and made deputy minister, he will now be appointed as the Istanbul Chief Public Prosecutor, he will appear later, as if the Deputy Minister's authority is limited, after saying that this "idiot" word of the judge was said to Süleyman Soylu, we will not talk about the judge who sentenced him for insulting the YSK delegation, and the prosecutors sent by Akın Gürlek are accused of charges that do not exist in the current Istanbul Metropolitan Municipality. We won't talk about file preparation, what will we talk about? Of course we will talk about this, we have to talk about these. We do not want a traveling delegation chairman at the Çağlayan Courthouse, we do not want an instruction from the palace and say "This decision will be made." Friends, we do not want judges or prosecutors who will file a lawsuit and make a decision. When a report was prepared about me as a member of parliament. I do not want a report to be filed against me because of the speeches I made in the Parliament, where I exercised my freedom of expression. As a member of parliament, I want to use my freedom of expression, and when citizens use it, they are shown the way to prison. Now “Trust the judiciary.” It is said, "Interference in the judiciary." It is said; Well, friends, I explained yesterday, are we, as the Republic of Türkiye, a member of the OECD? We are members. I told you about the OECD figure. According to the Organization for Economic Development and Co-operation's Government at a Glance Report - clear, I can send you the report if you want - while it was 59 percent in 2010, we are now 36th out of 38 members in terms of trust in the judiciary. This is a fact. We say: The judiciary should not be politicized; The government, the judiciary, the ruling party should not interfere with the judiciary.

Look, another intervention in the judiciary, the 2022 Türkiye Report explains that interference in the judiciary by the executive has become an established problem in Turkey. How can you deny this? Now, where can we say "No" to the fact that there is no intervention in the judiciary in the political cases filed? can you say? We are experiencing this, friends. This brother of yours has now had a summary of proceedings filed against him because he "retweeted" the "Tayyip world" cartoon in the 27th Term. Currently, lawsuits have been filed against 72 Republican People's Party MPs in the 27th term, from Ankara to Izmir, from Antalya to Trabzon, and a lawsuit has been filed for insulting the President. Could such a thing happen? I explained to you yesterday, you introduced the disinformation law, there is an investigation against 63 thousand people. Can there be a crime of insulting a party leader or the President in the Presidential government system other than the parliamentary system, which is called "insulting the President" - where the president of the political party is currently? We won't talk about these, where will we talk? Therefore, it is necessary to evaluate this article within this framework. If we do not evaluate this article, we cannot build the state of law in Turkey. We always say this: Democracy and freedoms. The future of a citizen of the Republic of Türkiye should not depend on the political decision of a prosecutor or a judge. There are hundreds of our citizens like Ekrem İmamoğlu who are wanted to be banned from politics. Has an investigation been carried out on the judge who decided to bring Kemal Kılıçdaroğlu by force? Believe my brother, he told me that the Istanbul Anatolian Chief Public Prosecutor, called İsmail Uçar, who is currently a member of the Supreme Court, came out and stated that judges and prosecutors took bribes in cases in the Anatolian Courthouse, and that they did not serve even a day in drug cases. What happened, what happened to those judges and prosecutors? Members of the Ministry of Justice, why are you silent? Just talk. Well, these will be discussed here, friends, in the Justice Commission, and will not be discussed elsewhere.
Dear President, dear members; Therefore, this article is clearly the politicization of the judiciary and the intervention of the politicized HSK, called "HSK", into the judiciary. This article is an article that brings the Presidential government system into a structure that does not recognize law and justice.
Therefore, we request the withdrawal of this article.

records.originalTurkish:
SÜLEYMAN BÜLBÜL (Aydın) - Teşekkür ediyorum Başkanım.
Bu düzenlemeler -hem 14 hem de 15 hakkında konuşuyorum- ilgili bölge adliye mahkemesindeki cumhuriyet savcıları arasından kıdemli olanı başsavcı vekili sayan uygulamayı kaldırmak, yerine HSK tarafından bu atamanın yapılacak olması, liyakat, deneyim, siyasallaşma endişesi, hukuk güvenliği, mesleki ilkeleri yönünden tartışılabilir bir hâle getirmek anlamına geliyor. Zira bu yeni başsavcı vekilleri siyasallaşmış bir HSK tarafından atanacakları gibi, diğer cumhuriyet başsavcılarının adli ve idari görevlerine ilişkin işlemlerini inceleyecek ve cumhuriyet başsavcısına bilgi verecektir.
Ayrıca teklifin gerekçesinde kıdemli cumhuriyet savcılarının emeklilik, tayin gibi nedenlerle görevden sık sık ayrılması bir sakınca sayılırken HSK’nin elinde bu başsavcı vekillerini sık sık değiştirme yetkisi de vardı. HSK’nin siyasallaşmış yapısı ve bugüne kadar kullandığı birçok atama ve görevden alma tercihini düşündüğümüzde, kritik konumdaki bir soruşturma, kovuşturma nedeniyle başsavcı vekillerinin sık sık değişmesi, bölgedeki kıdemli cumhuriyet savcılarının yeni baskılarla karşılaşmasını da mümkün hâle getirebilecektir. Bölgedeki en kıdemli cumhuriyet savcısının tecrübesi yerine, HSK’nin belirleyeceği birinci sınıftaki bir hâkim veya savcının bu konuma atanması, bazı yerler ve davalar üzerinde kamuoyunda yeni şüphelerin oluşması tehlikesini de içermektedir.
Sayın Başkan, ülkemizde yargının geldiği noktada -HSK’nin bulunduğu nokta- bizim için en büyük endişe yargının siyasallaşması. Yargının siyasallaşması demek, örneğin, Akın Gürlek’in Bakan Yardımcılığına gelmesi, Bakan Yardımcılığından sonra İstanbul Cumhuriyet Başsavcılığına gitmesi demektir. Yargının siyasallaşması demek, Hasan Yılmaz’ın -SBK’nin tedbir kararında talebi bulunan bir başsavcı, İstanbul Başsavcı Vekilinin- daha sonra Bakan Yardımcılığına getirilmesi -SBK kaçtıktan sonra- daha sonra Bakan Yardımcılığından Yargıtay üyeliğine getirilmesi demektir. Yani bu çerçevede bir gerçek var: Yargı siyasallaşıyor, HSK de bu siyasallaşmanın içerisinde bir kurum. Bunu ortadan kaldırmanın yollarını oluşturmamız gerekiyor yani somut nedenler açık ve net.
Şimdi, bizim Avrupa İnsan Hakları Mahkemesinde verilerimiz var. Bu verilere göre, Şubat 2024 sonu itibarıyla, AHİM’de bekleyen her 100 davadan 35’i Türk vatandaşlarının yaptığı başvurulardan oluşuyor. Şöyle baktığımız zaman, burada Avrupa İnsan Hakları Mahkemesinde bekleyen dosya sayılarının oranları var; bizim 100 davadan 35, Rusya 16, Ukrayna 12,9; Romanya 6; Yunanistan 3,6; İtalya 3,6; Sırbistan 2,3; Azerbaycan 3,1; diğer 37 ülke ise 13,2. Şimdi, Avrupa İnsan Hakları Mahkemesine üye olan 49 ülke çerçevesinde de düşündüğümüz zaman, bu 100 davadan 35 dava, Türkiye’deki yargıda adil yargılanma hakkının ihlal edildiğini açıkça ortaya koyuyor. Türkiye, yine, AHİM verilerine göre, 2023 yılında en çok adil yargılanma hakkının ihlali ve güvenlik ve özgürlük hakkının ihlali konularında mahkûm edilmiş durumda. Yani 2023 yılında adil yargılanma hakkının ihlali konusunda bir hak ihlali varsa demek ki yargıda bir şeyler ters gidiyor. Bu ters giden şeyler neler? Yine, Avrupa İnsan Hakları Mahkemesinin 2023 yılı itibarıyla en çok emsal karar verdiği ülke Türkiye arkadaşlar, emsal karar verilen ülke Türkiye. Uluslararası camiada 124 emsal dava Türkiye kararı olarak biliniyor. Şimdi, bir, 100 davadan 35’i; iki, adil yargılama hakkının ihlali ve güvenlik ve özgürlük hakkının ihlali; Türkiye’nin 2023 yılında AHİM’de; üç, en çok emsal karar verilen ülke de Türkiye, 124 Türkiye emsal kararı. Bu neyi gerektiriyor? Demek ki bizim ilk derece yargı ve üste Yargıtay, Danıştay gibi kurumlarda verilen kararların adil yargılanma hakkının ihlalinde... Yani iç hukuk yolunda hakkını alamayan Türkiye Cumhuriyeti vatandaşları var, hakkını alamıyor. Nereye gidiyor? Yargının en üst noktasına, Anayasa Mahkemesine, bireysel başvuru hakkını kullanıyor. Bireysel başvuru hakkını kullanmada da Türkiye 100 bin davayı aşmış durumda. Bu çerçevede de Adalet Bakanlığı çözümler arıyor bireysel başvuru hakkında. Demek ki bir sorun var. Bu sorunun nedenlerini araştırdığımız zaman, tabii ki yargının siyasallaşması.
Arkadaşlar, ilçelerden geliyoruz, illerden geliyoruz, ilçe başkanlıklarının ya da il başkanlıklarının ya da belirli partilerde siyaset içerisinde bulunanların yargıyı etkilemediğini kim söyleyebilir? Biz, 27’nci Dönemde ve 26’ncı Dönemde HSK’nin yapısıyla ve hâkim ve savcıların yapısıyla ilgili yaptığımız dönüşümlerde, bir siyasi partiye üye olan bir avukatın, hukuk fakültesi mezununun hâkim veya savcılığa geçebilmesine olanak sağlamadık mı? Sağladık. Nasıl oluyor bu iş? Bir hâkim veya savcı belediye başkanı adaylığına ya da milletvekilliğine aday olduğu zaman kazanamazsa geri dönmesi kolay oluyor mu? Olmuyor, hemen hemen geri dönemiyor. Ama bir siyasi partinin belediye Meclis üyesi, bir siyasi partinin üyesi, bir siyasi partinin il veya ilçe yöneticisi çıkıyor, hâkimlik sınavına giriyor, ondan sonra mülakattan sonra hâkim veya savcı oluyor. İşte, bizim endişelerimiz bunlar. Siz siyasi... Geçen dönem de yine seçim kurullarının oluşmasında hâkim ve savcıların kurayla kıdemli hâkim yerine getirilmesi konusunda da bizim endişelerimiz vardı. Bu endişelerimizin başında ne vardı? Bu siyasi parti üyesi olan hâkim, savcıların başvurusu ve kura yoluyla ya da kıdem esasını bir kenara bırakarak seçim kurullarının başına getirilmesiydi endişemiz. Oradaki endişelerimizi aynen tekrar ediyoruz. Yani yargı siyasallaşmıştır; hâkim, savcıların bir bölümü belirli siyasi parti üyeliklerinden gelen hâkim, savcılardır ve artık hâkimler, savcılar özellikle siyasi ve muhalif davalarda saraya bakarak karar vermektedir. Bu, açık ve nettir. Biraz önce Sayın İnan kardeşim...
ŞENGÜL KARSLI (İstanbul) - Açık ve net olan bir şey yok, itiraf edin.
SÜLEYMAN BÜLBÜL (Aydın) - Ama söylüyorum.
ŞENGÜL KARSLI (İstanbul) - Ama her şeyi...
SÜLEYMAN BÜLBÜL (Aydın) - Örnekleri söylüyorum.
ŞENGÜL KARSLI (İstanbul) - Bize örnek vermediniz, sadece genelleme yaptınız.
SÜLEYMAN BÜLBÜL (Aydın) - Bakın, isim veriyorum, Akın Gürlek olayını açık ve net söylüyorum. Bir, Selahattin Demirtaş davası; iki, Canan Kaftancıoğlu davası, üç; Sözcü davası; dört...
ŞENGÜL KARSLI (İstanbul) - Yargıyı etkilemeye çalışan asıl sizsiniz.
SÜLEYMAN BÜLBÜL (Aydın) - Ama devam ediyorum, bakın...
ŞENGÜL KARSLI (İstanbul) - Hâkimlerin kararlarını burada sorgulama hakkını buluyorsanız nasıl özgür ve bağımsız yargı...
SÜLEYMAN BÜLBÜL (Aydın) - Şengül Hanım, bakın... Ama ben örnek veriyorum size.
ŞENGÜL KARSLI (İstanbul) - Beğenmediğiniz dava sonuçlarının üzerinden bunun tarafsız olmayan bir tavır olduğunu nasıl iddia edebilirsiniz?
SÜLEYMAN BÜLBÜL (Aydın) - Çağlayan Adliyesinde gezici mahkeme reisiydi bu Akın Gürlek. Enis Berberoğlu davası... Bu davalarda gezici heyetin başkanı olarak...
ŞENGÜL KARSLI (İstanbul) - Sonuçlarını beğenmediniz diye bu, onun özgür olmadığı anlamına gelmez.
SÜLEYMAN BÜLBÜL (Aydın) - Burada Parlamento demek, konuşulan yer demektir; parlamenter de konuşan kişi demektir. Lütfen Şengül Hanım, lütfen, net söylüyorum size.
ŞENGÜL KARSLI (İstanbul) - Doğruyu söylemek de onun görevidir. Ama sizin söylediğiniz...
İNAN AKGÜN ALP (Kars) - Bütün FETÖ’cü hâkim ve savcılar kararlarını böyle savundu zamanında. Bütün FETÖ’cü hâkimler aynı savunmayı yapıyordu. “Yargı özgürdür.” diyordu, “Bağımsızdır.” diyordu, “Kimse bu kararları tartışmasın.” diyordu. Şimdi geldiniz, aynı şeyi kendiniz yapıyorsunuz.
SÜLEYMAN BÜLBÜL (Aydın) - Ekrem İmamoğlu davasında hâkimi Samsun’a süren ben miyim? Lütfen, lütfen...
BAŞKAN CÜNEYT YÜKSEL - Sayın milletvekilleri, sayın milletvekilleri... Söz isteyin lütfen.
ŞENGÜL KARSLI (İstanbul) - “Hâkimler tarafsız, bağımsız olmalı.” diyorsunuz, verdikleri kararları yargılıyorsunuz. Sizin beğenmediğiniz kararlar...
İNAN AKGÜN ALP (Kars) - Kararı yargılamıyoruz, biz atamayı konuşuyoruz.
BAŞKAN CÜNEYT YÜKSEL - Sayın Karslı... Sayın Alp...
SÜLEYMAN BÜLBÜL (Aydın) - Biz Parlamentodayız Sayın Karslı...
FAHRETTİN TUĞRUL (Uşak) -Ne bağırıyorsun ya, niye bağırıyorsun?
BAŞKAN CÜNEYT YÜKSEL - Sayın Alp...
FAHRETTİN TUĞRUL (Uşak) -Neden bağırıyorsun ya?
İNAN AKGÜN ALP (Kars) - Öyle müdahale edemezsin. Bağırırsan bağırırım. “Neden bağırıyorsun?” diye önce vekiline söyleyeceksin.
FAHRETTİN TUĞRUL (Uşak) -Sakin ol ya!
BAŞKAN CÜNEYT YÜKSEL - Arkadaşlar, arkadaşlar, Sayın Bülbül’ün konuşmasına izin verin.
FAHRETTİN TUĞRUL (Uşak) -Karşınızda bir kadın milletvekili var, saygılı olun. Kadın hakları diye bağırıp duruyorsunuz. Karşınızda bir kadın milletvekili var, saygılı olun, bağırıp duruyorsun!
İNAN AKGÜN ALP (Kars) - Milletvekiliyle konuşuyorum ben, kadın-erkek davası değil bu.
FAHRETTİN TUĞRUL (Uşak) -Burası bağıracak yer değil.
İNAN AKGÜN ALP (Kars) - Ne demek “Bağıracak yer değil.”
ŞENGÜL KARSLI (İstanbul) - Adalete olan güveni zedelemek, bu topluma yaptığınız en büyük zarardır, haksızlıktır. Bunu da hoyratça yapmayın.
BAŞKAN CÜNEYT YÜKSEL - Sayın Karslı, söz vereceğim size.
SÜLEYMAN BÜLBÜL (Aydın) - Arkadaşlar, dediğim gibi, Parlamento demek, konuşulan yerdir, parlamenter demek de konuşan kişidir. Biz halkın iradesini, vatandaşın adaletsizlik çığlıklarını, hukuk devletinin ihlallerini burada söylemek zorundayız. Biz burada söylemeyeceğiz, nerede söyleyeceğiz arkadaşlar, nereye söyleyeceğiz?
NURETTİN ALAN (İstanbul) - Sayın Bülbül, görülmekte olan davalar da konuşulmaz. Anayasa 138’de, görülmekte olan davalarda Parlamentonun...
SÜLEYMAN BÜLBÜL (Aydın) - Sayın Alan, onlar geçen davalar, geçen.
İNAN AKGÜN ALP (Kars) - Görülmekte olan dava değil onun bahsettiği, bir başsavcı atama kararından bahsediyor, görülmekte olan bir dava değil ve başsavcının daha önceki yaptıklarından bahsediyor. İkisi farklı şey, ikisini birbirine karıştırmayın; siyasi atamadan bahsediyor.
SÜLEYMAN BÜLBÜL (Aydın) - Arkadaşlar...
NURETTİN ALAN (İstanbul) - İmamoğlu davasından bahsediyor.
İNAN AKGÜN ALP (Kars) - Siyasi saikle yapılan yargısal müdahaleden bahsediyor. Onun konuşulma...
BAŞKAN CÜNEYT YÜKSEL - Tamam Sayın Alan, karşılıklı konuşmayalım lütfen.
SÜLEYMAN BÜLBÜL (Aydın) - Arkadaşlar...
BAŞKAN CÜNEYT YÜKSEL - Sayın Alan, söz vereceğim size.
SÜLEYMAN BÜLBÜL (Aydın) - Arkadaşlar, bu madde 14 ve 15’inci maddedir. 14’üncü ve 15’inci maddede ne vardır? Cumhuriyet başsavcı vekillerinin BAM’a atanmasıyla ilgili yeni bir düzenleme vardır. Nedir bugüne kadar? BAM’da kıdemli olanlar başsavcı vekili oluyordu, şimdi ise “HSK tarafından atanması” deniyor. Birinci sınıfa ayrılmış ya da birinci sınıfa ayrılma nitelikleri olan, BAM’da çalışmamış olan savcılar belirli bir süre savcılık yapmışsa BAM’a atanacak arkadaşlar. Kim atayacak? HSK atayacak. Kıdemi bir kenara itmişsiniz. Burada liyakat diyoruz, yargının siyasallaşmasındaki endişelerimizi ortaya koyuyoruz. Burada bunu konuşmayacağız, Akın Gürlek’i konuşmayacağız, gezici heyetin başkanı, Enis Berberoğlu davasında verilen “Anayasa Mahkemesi kararını tanımıyorum.” diyen yargıcı konuşmayacağız, Çağlayan Adliyesindeki 7 tane siyasi davada gezici heyetin başkanını konuşmayacağız ve ödüllendirilip de bakan yardımcısı yapıldıktan sonra bu kişi girecek, şu anda İstanbul Cumhuriyet Başsavcısı olarak atanacak, Bakan Yardımcılığı yetkisi az kalmış gibi, daha sonra çıkacak, hâkimin bu “ahmak” lafı Süleyman Soylu’ya söylenmiştir dedikten sonra, YSK heyetine hakaretten dolayı ceza veren hâkim konusunda konuşmayacağız ve Akın Gürlek’in gönderdiği savcıların şu andaki İstanbul Büyükşehir Belediyesinde olmayan suçlamalarla dosya hazırlamasını konuşmayacağız, neyi konuşacağız? Tabii ki konuşacağız bunu, bunları konuşmak zorundayız. Biz Çağlayan Adliyesinde gezici heyet başkanı istemiyoruz, saraydan talimat verilip de “Şu karar verilecek.” diye dava açacak, karar verecek hâkim, savcı istemiyoruz arkadaşlar. Ben bir milletvekili olarak hakkımda fezleke düzenlendiği zaman... İfade özgürlüğümü kullandığım Mecliste yaptığım konuşmalardan dolayı hakkımda fezleke düzenlemesini istemiyorum. Bir milletvekili olarak ben ifade özgürlüğümü kullanmak istiyorum ki vatandaş kullandığı zaman cezaevi yolu gösteriliyor. Şimdi “Yargıya güven.” deniliyor, “Yargıya müdahale.” deniyor; yahu arkadaşlar, dün açıkladım, dün, OECD’ye üye miyiz biz Türkiye Cumhuriyeti olarak? Üyeyiz. OECD rakamından bahsettim size, Ekonomik Kalkınma ve İşbirliği Örgütünün Bir Bakışta Hükûmet Raporu’na göre -net, istiyorsanız göndereyim raporu da- 2010’da yüzde 59 iken şu anda 38 üye arasında 36’ncı olmuşuz yargıya güvende. Bu bir gerçek. Diyoruz ki biz: Yargı siyasallaşmasın; Hükûmet, yargı, iktidar yargıya müdahale etmesin. Bakın, bir tane daha yargıya müdahale, 2022 Türkiye Raporu’nda Türkiye’de yürütme tarafından yargıya müdahale edilmesinin yerleşik bir sorun hâline geldiğini açıklıyor. Bunu nasıl inkâr edebilirsiniz? Şimdi, açılan siyasi davalarda yargıya müdahale edilmediğine nerede “Hayır.” diyebilirsiniz? Biz bunu yaşıyoruz arkadaşlar. Bu kardeşiniz 27’nci Dönemde “Tayyipler âlemi” karikatürünü “retweet”lediğinden dolayı şu anda hakkında fezleke düzenlendi. 27’nci Dönemdeki 72 Cumhuriyet Halk Partisi milletvekili hakkında şu anda Ankara’dan İzmir’e, Antalya’dan Trabzon’a kadar haklarında davalar açıldı, Cumhurbaşkanına hakaret davası açıldı. Böyle şey olabilir mi? Dün açıkladım size ben, dezenformasyon yasasını getirdiniz, 63 bin kişi hakkında soruşturma var. “Cumhurbaşkanına hakaret” denilen -şu anda siyasi partinin başkanı olan- parlamenter sistem dışında Cumhurbaşkanlığı hükûmet sisteminde bir parti başkanına, Cumhurbaşkanına hakaret suçu olabilir mi? Biz bunları konuşmayacağız, nerede konuşacağız? Bu nedenle, bu çerçevede bu maddeyi değerlendirmek lazım. Bu maddeyi değerlendirmezsek Türkiye’de hukuk devletini inşa edemeyiz. Biz her zaman şunu söylüyoruz: Demokrasi ve özgürlükler. Bir Türkiye Cumhuriyeti vatandaşının geleceği bir savcının, bir hâkimin siyasal vereceği karara bağlı olmamalı. Ekrem İmamoğlu gibi yüzlerce vatandaşımız var siyasi yasaklı yapılmak istenen. Kemal Kılıçdaroğlu hakkında -yine söylüyorum- zorla getirme kararı veren hâkim hakkında bir çalışma yapıldı mı? İnan kardeşim söyledi, bu İsmail Uçar denilen, şu anda Yargıtay üyesi olan İstanbul Anadolu Cumhuriyet Başsavcısı çıkıp da hâkimlerin ve savcıların Anadolu Adliyesinde davalarda rüşvet aldığını, uyuşturucu davalarında bir gün dahi yatmadıklarını belirtti. Ne oldu, ne oldu o hâkim ve savcılara? Adalet Bakanlığı mensupları niye susuyorsunuz? Konuşsanıza. İşte, bunlar burada konuşulacak arkadaşlar, Adalet Komisyonunda konuşulacak, başka yerlerde konuşulmayacak.
Sayın Başkanım, sayın üyelerim; bu nedenle bu madde açıkça yargının siyasallaşması ve “HSK” denen siyasallaşan HSK’nin yargıya müdahalesinin açık maddesidir. Bu madde Cumhurbaşkanlığı hükûmet sisteminin geldiği hukuk tanımaz, adalet tanımaz bir yapının getirildiği bir maddedir.
Bu nedenle, bu maddenin geri çekilmesini talep ediyoruz.
records.englishTranslation:
SÜLEYMAN BÜLBÜL (Aydın) - Thank you, President.
These regulations - I'm talking about both 14 and 15 - mean removing the practice that considers the senior public prosecutor in the relevant regional court of justice to be the deputy chief prosecutor, and instead making this appointment by the HSK debatable in terms of merit, experience, concern about politicization, legal security, and professional principles. Because these new deputy chief prosecutors will be appointed by a politicized HSK, they will examine the actions of other chief public prosecutors regarding their judicial and administrative duties and inform the chief public prosecutor.
In addition, in the justification of the proposal, while it was considered a drawback for senior public prosecutors to leave office frequently due to reasons such as retirement or appointment, the HSK also had the authority to change these deputy chief prosecutors frequently. Considering the politicized structure of the HSK and the many appointment and dismissal choices it has used to date, the frequent change of deputy chief prosecutors due to a critical investigation and prosecution may make it possible for senior public prosecutors in the region to face new pressures. Appointing a first-class judge or prosecutor determined by the HSK to this position, instead of the experience of the most senior public prosecutor in the region, also carries the danger of creating new doubts in the public opinion on some places and cases.
Mr. President, at the point where the judiciary is in our country - where HSK is at - the biggest concern for us is the politicization of the judiciary. Politicization of the judiciary means, for example, Akın Gürlek becoming Deputy Minister, and after being Deputy Minister, he goes to the Istanbul Chief Public Prosecutor's Office. Politicization of the judiciary means that Hasan Yılmaz - a chief prosecutor who had a request in the injunction decision of the SBK, the Deputy Chief Public Prosecutor of Istanbul - was later appointed to the position of Deputy Minister - after the SBK escaped - and then from the position of Deputy Minister to the member of the Supreme Court of Appeals. So, there is a fact in this context: The judiciary is becoming politicized, and HSK is an institution within this politicization. We need to create ways to eliminate this, so the concrete reasons are clear.
Now, we have data in the European Court of Human Rights. According to these data, as of the end of February 2024, 35 out of every 100 cases pending at ECHR consist of applications made by Turkish citizens. When we look at it this way, here are the ratios of the number of files pending in the European Court of Human Rights; our 35 out of 100 cases, Russia 16, Ukraine 12.9; Romania 6; Greece 3.6; Italy 3.6; Serbia 2.3; Azerbaijan 3.1; The other 37 countries are 13.2. Now, when we consider the 49 countries that are members of the European Court of Human Rights, 35 cases out of these 100 clearly demonstrate that the right to a fair trial has been violated in the judiciary in Turkey. Again, according to ECHR data, Türkiye has been convicted mostly for violation of the right to a fair trial and violation of the right to security and freedom in 2023. So, if there is a violation of the right to a fair trial in 2023, it means that something is wrong in the judiciary. What are these things going wrong?

Again, Türkiye is the country where the European Court of Human Rights has given the most precedent decisions as of 2023, friends, the country where precedent decisions have been made is Türkiye. In the international community, 124 precedent cases are known as Türkiye decisions. Now, one, 35 out of 100 cases; two, violation of the right to a fair trial and violation of the right to security and liberty; Turkey in AHİM in 2023; Three, the country with the most precedent decisions is Türkiye, 124 Turkish precedent decisions. What does this entail? This means that the decisions made in our first instance judiciary and institutions such as the Supreme Court and the Council of State are in violation of the right to a fair trial. In other words, there are citizens of the Republic of Türkiye who cannot get their rights through domestic law. Where is it going? He exercises his right to individual application to the highest point of the judiciary, the Constitutional Court. Türkiye has exceeded 100 thousand cases in exercising the right to individual application. In this context, the Ministry of Justice is looking for solutions regarding individual applications. So there is a problem. When we investigate the reasons for this problem, it is, of course, the politicization of the judiciary.
Friends, we come from districts, we come from provinces, who can say that district presidencies, provincial presidencies or those in politics in certain parties do not influence the judiciary? In the transformations we made regarding the structure of the HSK and the structure of judges and prosecutors in the 27th and 26th Term, did we not enable a lawyer who is a member of a political party or a law school graduate to become a judge or prosecutor? We provided. How does this work? When a judge or prosecutor runs for mayor or a member of parliament and does not win, is it easy for him to return? It doesn't work, he almost can't come back. But he becomes a municipal council member of a political party, a member of a political party, a provincial or district manager of a political party, takes the judgeship exam, and then becomes a judge or prosecutor after the interview. Here, these are our concerns. You are political. Last term, we also had concerns about judges and prosecutors being appointed as senior judges by lottery in the formation of election boards. What was our primary concern? Our concern was that this judge, who is a member of a political party, was appointed to head the election boards upon the application of prosecutors and through lottery or by setting aside the principle of seniority. We repeat our concerns there exactly. In other words, the judiciary has become politicized; Some of the judges and prosecutors are judges and prosecutors who come from certain political party memberships, and now judges and prosecutors make decisions, especially in political and opposition cases, by looking at the palace. This is clear and obvious. Just now, dear brother İnan.
ŞENGÜL KARSLI (Istanbul) - There is nothing clear, admit it.
SÜLEYMAN BÜLBÜL (Aydın) - But I'm telling you.
ŞENGÜL KARSLI (Istanbul) - But everything.
SÜLEYMAN BÜLBÜL (Aydın) - I am giving examples.
ŞENGÜL KARSLI (Istanbul) - You didn't give us any examples, you just made generalizations.
SÜLEYMAN BÜLBÜL (Aydın) - Look, I am giving names, I am telling the Akın Gürlek incident clearly. One, Selahattin Demirtaş case; two, Canan Kaftancıoğlu case, three; Spokesman case; four.
ŞENGÜL KARSLI (Istanbul) - You are the one trying to influence the judiciary.
SÜLEYMAN BÜLBÜL (Aydın) - But I continue, look.

ŞENGÜL KARSLI (Istanbul) - If you have the right to question the decisions of judges here, what kind of a free and independent judiciary is?
SÜLEYMAN BÜLBÜL (Aydın) - Ms. Şengül, look. But I'm giving you an example.
ŞENGÜL KARSLI (Istanbul) - How can you claim that this is a non-neutral attitude based on the results of the case that you do not like?
SÜLEYMAN BÜLBÜL (Aydın) - This was Akın Gürlek, the head of the mobile court at Çağlayan Courthouse. Enis Berberoğlu case. As the chairman of the traveling committee in these cases.
ŞENGÜL KARSLI (Istanbul) - Just because you do not like the results does not mean that it is not free.
SÜLEYMAN BÜLBÜL (Aydın) - Parliament here means the place where people talk; Parliamentarian also means the person who speaks. Please Ms. Şengül, please, I'm telling you clearly.
ŞENGÜL KARSLI (Istanbul) - It is also his duty to tell the truth. But what you said.
İNAN AKGÜN ALP (Kars) - All FETO-supporting judges and prosecutors defended their decisions like this at the time. All FETO judges were making the same defense. “The judiciary is free.” he said, “It is independent.” "No one should discuss these decisions," he said. he said. Now you've come, you're doing the same thing yourself.
SÜLEYMAN BÜLBÜL (Aydın) - Am I the one who sent the judge in the Ekrem İmamoğlu case to Samsun? Please, please.
MAYOR CÜNEYT Yüksel - Dear MPs, dear MPs. Please ask for a word.
ŞENGÜL KARSLI (Istanbul) - “Judges should be impartial and independent.” You say, you judge the decisions they make. Decisions you don't like.
İNAN AKGÜN ALP (Kars) - We are not judging the decision, we are talking about the appointment.
MAYOR CÜNEYT Yüksel - Mr. Karslı. Mr Alp.
SÜLEYMAN BÜLBÜL (Aydın) - We are in the Parliament, Mr. Karslı.
FAHRETTIN TUĞRUL (Uşak) -What are you shouting, why are you shouting?
CHAIRMAN CÜNEYT Yüksel - Mr. Alp.
FAHRETTIN TUĞRUL (Uşak) -Why are you shouting?
İNAN AKGÜN ALP (Kars) - You cannot intervene like that. If you shout, I will shout. “Why are you shouting?” You will tell your deputy first.
FAHRETTİN TUĞRUL (Uşak) - Calm down!
MAYOR CÜNEYT YÜKSEL - Friends, friends, let Mr. Bülbül speak.
FAHRETTİN TUĞRUL (Uşak) -There is a female MP in front of you, be respectful. You keep shouting about women's rights. There is a female MP in front of you, be respectful, you keep shouting!
İNAN AKGÜN ALP (Kars) - I am talking to the MP, this is not a case between men and women.
FAHRETTİN TUĞRUL (Uşak) -This is not the place to shout.
İNAN AKGÜN ALP (Kars) - What do you mean, “This is not the place to shout?”
ŞENGÜL KARSLI (Istanbul) - Damaging the trust in justice is the biggest harm you do to this society, it is injustice. Don't do this rudely either.
MAYOR CÜNEYT Yüksel - Dear Karslı, I will give you my word.
SÜLEYMAN BÜLBÜL (Aydın) - Friends, as I said, Parliament means the place where people talk, and parliamentarian means the person who speaks. We have to express the will of the people, the citizens' screams of injustice, and the violations of the rule of law here. We won't say it here, where will we say it, friends, where will we say it?
NURETTİN ALAN (Istanbul) - Dear Bülbül, ongoing cases cannot be discussed. In Article 138 of the Constitution, the Parliament in pending cases.
SÜLEYMAN BÜLBÜL (Aydın) - Dear Alan, these are the cases that have passed.

İNAN AKGÜN ALP (Kars) - What he is talking about is not the current case, he is talking about the decision to appoint a chief prosecutor. It is not an ongoing case and he is talking about the prosecutor general's previous actions. They are two different things, do not confuse them; He's talking about political appointment.
SÜLEYMAN BÜLBÜL (Aydın) - Friends.
NURETTİN ALAN (Istanbul) - Talking about the İmamoğlu case.
İNAN AKGÜN ALP (Kars) - He is talking about politically motivated judicial intervention. Don't talk about it.
CHAIRMAN CÜNEYT YÜKSEL - Okay, Mr. Alan, let's not talk to each other, please.
SÜLEYMAN BÜLBÜL (Aydın) - Friends.
MAYOR CÜNEYT Yüksel - Dear Alan, I will give you my word.
SÜLEYMAN BÜLBÜL (Aydın) - Friends, this article is the 14th and 15th article. What is in the 14th and 15th articles? There is a new regulation regarding the appointment of deputy chief public prosecutors to BAM. What is it so far? Those who were senior in BAM became deputy chief prosecutors, but now it is said that they are "appointed by the HSK". Prosecutors who are first class or have first class qualifications and who have not worked at BAM will be appointed to BAM if they have been prosecutors for a certain period of time. Who will appoint? HSK will appoint. You pushed seniority aside. We say merit here, we reveal our concerns about the politicization of the judiciary. We will not talk about this here, we will not talk about Akın Gürlek, the head of the traveling delegation said, "I do not recognize the decision of the Constitutional Court" given in the Enis Berberoğlu case. We will not talk about the judge who said, we will not talk about the head of the traveling delegation in 7 political cases in the Çağlayan Courthouse, and this person will enter after being rewarded and made deputy minister, he will now be appointed as the Istanbul Chief Public Prosecutor, he will appear later, as if the Deputy Minister's authority is limited, after saying that this "idiot" word of the judge was said to Süleyman Soylu, we will not talk about the judge who sentenced him for insulting the YSK delegation, and the prosecutors sent by Akın Gürlek are accused of charges that do not exist in the current Istanbul Metropolitan Municipality. We won't talk about file preparation, what will we talk about? Of course we will talk about this, we have to talk about these. We do not want a traveling delegation chairman at the Çağlayan Courthouse, we do not want an instruction from the palace and say "This decision will be made." Friends, we do not want judges or prosecutors who will file a lawsuit and make a decision. When a report was prepared about me as a member of parliament. I do not want a report to be filed against me because of the speeches I made in the Parliament, where I exercised my freedom of expression. As a member of parliament, I want to use my freedom of expression, and when citizens use it, they are shown the way to prison. Now “Trust the judiciary.” It is said, "Interference in the judiciary." It is said; Well, friends, I explained yesterday, are we, as the Republic of Türkiye, a member of the OECD? We are members. I told you about the OECD figure. According to the Organization for Economic Development and Co-operation's Government at a Glance Report - clear, I can send you the report if you want - while it was 59 percent in 2010, we are now 36th out of 38 members in terms of trust in the judiciary. This is a fact. We say: The judiciary should not be politicized; The government, the judiciary, the ruling party should not interfere with the judiciary.

Look, another intervention in the judiciary, the 2022 Türkiye Report explains that interference in the judiciary by the executive has become an established problem in Turkey. How can you deny this? Now, where can we say "No" to the fact that there is no intervention in the judiciary in the political cases filed? can you say? We are experiencing this, friends. This brother of yours has now had a summary of proceedings filed against him because he "retweeted" the "Tayyip world" cartoon in the 27th Term. Currently, lawsuits have been filed against 72 Republican People's Party MPs in the 27th term, from Ankara to Izmir, from Antalya to Trabzon, and a lawsuit has been filed for insulting the President. Could such a thing happen? I explained to you yesterday, you introduced the disinformation law, there is an investigation against 63 thousand people. Can there be a crime of insulting a party leader or the President in the Presidential government system other than the parliamentary system, which is called "insulting the President" - where the president of the political party is currently? We won't talk about these, where will we talk? Therefore, it is necessary to evaluate this article within this framework. If we do not evaluate this article, we cannot build the state of law in Turkey. We always say this: Democracy and freedoms. The future of a citizen of the Republic of Türkiye should not depend on the political decision of a prosecutor or a judge. There are hundreds of our citizens like Ekrem İmamoğlu who are wanted to be banned from politics. Has an investigation been carried out on the judge who decided to bring Kemal Kılıçdaroğlu by force? Believe my brother, he told me that the Istanbul Anatolian Chief Public Prosecutor, called İsmail Uçar, who is currently a member of the Supreme Court, came out and stated that judges and prosecutors took bribes in cases in the Anatolian Courthouse, and that they did not serve even a day in drug cases. What happened, what happened to those judges and prosecutors? Members of the Ministry of Justice, why are you silent? Just talk. Well, these will be discussed here, friends, in the Justice Commission, and will not be discussed elsewhere.
Dear President, dear members; Therefore, this article is clearly the politicization of the judiciary and the intervention of the politicized HSK, called "HSK", into the judiciary. This article is an article that brings the Presidential government system into a structure that does not recognize law and justice.
Therefore, we request the withdrawal of this article.
